footer.description: a WCAG contrast checker for devs and designers who care about accessibility.

hero.title: COLOR CONTRAST CHECKER
hero.subtitle: Calculate the contrast ratio of text and background colors.

checkCondition.noteSmall: Small text (below 18pt) requires a minimum ratio of 4.5:1 for good contrast.
checkCondition.noteLarge: Large text (above 18pt or bold above 14pt) requires a minimum ratio of 3:1.
checkCondition.noteUi: UI components and icons require a minimum ratio of 3:1.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.whatIsBody: Color contrast is the difference in luminance between two colors — typically text and its background. A higher contrast ratio means text is easier to read, especially for people with visual impairments. The contrast ratio ranges from 1:1 (identical colors) to 21:1 (black on white).
howItWorks.step01: Pick your foreground color — the color of your text or icon.
howItWorks.step02: Pick your background color — the surface behind your text.
howItWorks.step03: The contrast ratio is calculated instantly and displayed in real time.
howItWorks.step04: Check the table to see which WCAG levels your combination passes or fails.
howItWorks.wcagBody: WCAG (Web Content Accessibility Guidelines) defines two levels of contrast compliance:
howItWorks.aaRule2: Large text (above 18pt or bold above 14pt) — minimum ratio of 3:1
howItWorks.aaRule3: UI components and icons — minimum ratio of 3:1
